goal: Sub 1:50 (chase 1:45) · Kiprun Singapore Half Marathon · 27 Sep 2026
health_notes: Post-illness recovery (fever 38°C, body aches, cough 16–20 Jun). Two clean runs done 25 Jun and 29 Jun.
Knee niggle (medial, mild) flared once mid-June — resolved with rest. Watch for recurrence.
VO2 Max dipped post-illness but still top 5% for age/gender — expect full return.
key_context: Gym (Snap Fitness Buangkok) opens August — literally across the road.
Columbia OutDry hiking shoes need 50–80km break-in before Norway (Aug 8). Must be worn for all walks and stair sessions from now. Blisters on Day 1 would be catastrophic.

Norway prep (urgent — active NOW through Aug 8): (1) Shoe break-in walks 3–4x/week in Columbia OutDry hiking shoes (need 50–80km before Aug 8); (2) Stair climbing with 7–10kg loaded pack 2–3x/week from Jul 19 — simulate the 600–1000m daily elevation gains; (3) Single-leg step-downs 3x10 after every run for eccentric quad strength (downhill protection); (4) Train consecutive hard days — if stairs Saturday, do stairs Sunday too (Aug 10–11 are back-to-back 1000m+ gain days). Do at least one full loaded gear walk (2–3hr) before Aug 8.

Norway prep WHERE (Singapore locations): loaded stair sessions — HDB stairwell (own block, free/weatherproof, best for repeat vertical), Bukit Timah Hill (163m + metal staircase, real downhill), Mount Faber / Telok Blangah / Southern Ridges (rolling stairs, good for long 2–3hr loaded gear walk). Step-downs — any step/bench at home after runs. Gym (Snap Fitness Buangkok, opens Aug) — step-ups, walking lunges, split squats, calf raises for extra eccentric load. Prescribe these in the note's Norway Prep section alongside runs; stack loaded stairs on easy/non-quality run days to protect tempo quality.

Shoe rotation: (1) Asics Gel-Kayano 31 — heavy max-stability cushioned daily trainer — use for EASY runs, LONG runs, recovery days; (2) Dynafish Xiaonian — lightweight ~190g plateless TPEE super-trainer, bouncy/responsive — use for TEMPO, THRESHOLD, faster efforts, strides. Rotating both reduces injury risk. NOTE: Xiaonian has high stack + soft foam (less stable); given the mild knee niggle history, ease into it over a few shorter runs before hard tempo. Recommend the appropriate shoe per session in the note.
IPPT target: Silver Nov 2026, Gold Feb 2027.
Daily habits: stairs up on return home, push-ups + sit-ups morning routine (resume once fully recovered).

Norway (8–22 Aug): 9 hike days, ~83km total, ~6,059m elevation gain across Senja and Lofoten. ALL hikes rated Hard (one Moderate-Hard). Full schedule: Aug 9 Husfjellet 8.2km/640m; Aug 10 Segla 4.4km/610m + Hesten 5.3km/545m (two hikes same day); Aug 11 Grytetippen+Keipen 8.9km/1002m; Aug 14 Matmora+Delpen 9.7km/835m; Aug 15 Reinebringen 6km/448m; Aug 17 Munkebu+Munken 13km/800m; Aug 18 Ryten+Kvalvika 7.6km/597m; Aug 19 Haen via Hornet 10.3km/582m. Rest days: Aug 8 (travel/arrival), Aug 12-13, Aug 16, Aug 20-22 (travel). Aug 10 and Aug 11 are consecutive hard days with high elevation. This is serious aerobic work — treat as high-volume low-intensity cross-training, NOT a deload. Expect significant leg fatigue during but strong aerobic adaptation after.
July 2026 mileage goal: 100km running. Plan sessions to hit this target while staying injury-free.

ONE-OFF UPDATE (22 Jul): Reuben's evening freed up — an EASY run is on for tonight (Wed 22 Jul). Keep tempo on Thursday 23 Jul (HRV eased 3 nights: 88→85→78ms, readiness 59 MODERATE — do NOT move tempo forward; run it fresh Thursday). Today = easy 5–6km HR≤135 Kayano 31; Thursday = tempo Dynafish Xiaonian; Friday = easy. All other days open.

hr_zones.notes: Singapore heat — expect 5–8 bpm higher than cool-weather equivalent
